title: Savory Sweet Thai Crustaceans Som Tum
description: A yeasty, fluffy, and savory Thai dish featuring crustaceans, ssam-wrap, and flexitarian-friendly ingredients, perfect for a midnight craving. This central-Thai inspired recipe combines the freshness of som tum with the richness of crustaceans.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine yeast, flour, and a pinch of salt, then gradually add coconut milk and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rest in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Cook the dough circles for 2 minutes on each side, or until they are lightly browned and crispy.
In a separate pan, heat fish sauce, lime juice, chili flakes, and palm sugar over low heat, stirring until the sugar has dissolved.
Add the shrimp to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they are pink and fully cooked.
To assemble the som tum, place a cooked dough circle on a lettuce leaf, top with a shrimp, and spoon some of the sauce over the top.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
